questions.q: The equation of state for $\mathrm{n}$ moles of an ideal gas is $\mathrm{PV}=\mathrm{nRT}$, where $\mathrm{R}$ is a constant. The SI unit for $\mathrm{R}$ is
questions.solution: $\mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: An ideal diatomic gas with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>v</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi>R</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> occupies a volume <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>V</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> at a pressure <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> . The gas undergoes a process in which the pressure is proportional to the volume. At the end of process the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>rms</mi></math> speed of the gas molecules has doubled from its initial value then the heat supplied to the gas in the given process is
questions.solution:
As P∝V

∴PV-1=constant

Also, C=Cv-Rx-1=52R-R-1-1=3R

But as rms speed is doubled therefore temperature becomes four times.

Hence, Q=nc∆T=n×3R×3T1=9nRTi

=9 P1V1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A monoatomic gas of molar mass $m$ is kept in a insulated container. Container is moving with velocity $v$. If the container is suddenly stopped, then the change in the temperature of the gas is
questions.solution:
Let the insulated container has $n$ moles of the monoatomic gas. The loss of kinetic energy of the gas,
$\Delta E_K=\frac{1}{2}(m n) v^2$
If the change in temperature of the gas is $\Delta T$, then heat gained by the gas is,
$\Delta Q=\frac{3}{2} n R \Delta T$
Now, according to question,
$\begin{aligned}
\Delta Q & =\Delta E_K \\
\frac{3}{2} n R \Delta T & =\frac{1}{2} m n v^2 \\
\Rightarrow \quad \Delta T & =\frac{m v^2}{3 R}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: When heat <math><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi></math> is supplied to a diatomic gas of rigid molecules, at constant volume its temperature increases by <math><mi> </mi><mo>∆</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> . The heat required to produce the same change in temperature, at a constant pressure is:
questions.solution:
For diatomic gas CP=72R
CV=52R
Heat supplied at constant volume Q=nCVΔT
Heat supplied at constant pressure Q'=nCPΔT
QQ'=CVCP
Q'=QCPCV=75Q
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The equation of state for $5 \mathrm{~g}$ of oxygen at a pressure $P$ and temperature $T$, when occupying a volume $V$, will be:
(where $R$ is the gas constant)
questions.solution:
We know that $P V=\underline{n} R T$
$\begin{gathered}
\therefore n=\frac{m}{\text { moleculer mass }}=\frac{5}{32} \\
\Rightarrow \quad P V=\left(\frac{5}{32}\right) R T
\end{gathered}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The speed of sound in an ideal gas at a given temperature $T$ is $v$. The rms speed of gas molecules at that temperature is $v_{\mathrm{rms}}$. The ratio of the velocities $v$ and $v_{\text {rms }}$ for helium and oxygen gases are $X$ and $X^{\prime}$. respectively. Then, $\frac{X}{X^{\prime}}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
The speed of sound at a given temperature is
given by
$v_{\text {sound }}=\sqrt{\frac{r R T}{M}} \Rightarrow v_{\mathrm{rms}}=\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M}}$
For oxygen molecules; $\frac{v_0}{v_{\text {rms }}}=\frac{\sqrt{\frac{r_0 R T}{M}}}{\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M}}}=\sqrt{\frac{r_0}{3}}$
For helium molecules; $\frac{v_{\mathrm{He}}}{v_{\mathrm{rms}}}=\frac{\sqrt{r_{\mathrm{He}} \frac{R T}{M}}}{\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M}}}=\sqrt{\frac{r_{\mathrm{He}}}{3}}$ $\left(r_{\mathrm{Be}}=\frac{5}{3}, r_{\mathrm{O}}=\frac{7}{5}\right)$
$\frac{x^{\prime}}{x}=\frac{\sqrt{\frac{5}{9}}}{\sqrt{\frac{7}{15}}}=\sqrt{\frac{25}{21}}=\frac{5}{\sqrt{21}}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The temperature of an open room of volume <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> increases from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>17</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> due to the sunshine. The atmospheric pressure in the room remains <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>5</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>Pa</mi></math>. If <math><msub><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>i</mi></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>f</mi></mrow></msub></math> are the number of molecules in the room before and after heating, then <math><msub><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>f</mi></mrow></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>i</mi></mrow></msub></math> will be:
questions.solution:
Ti=273+17=290K

Tf=273+27=300K

From ideal gas equation, PV=nRT ⇒n=PVRT

nf-ni=PVR 1Tf-1Ti= -2.5×1025
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Assertion : Vibrational energy of diatomic molecule corresponding to each degree of freedom is $k_B T$.Reason : For every molecule, vibrational degree of freedom is 2 .
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: According to law of equipartition of energy, in thermal equilibrium, at temperature $T$, each degree of freedom of translational, rotational and vibrational motion contributes an average energy equal to $\frac{1}{2} k_B T$. The vibrational motion has two types of energy associated with the vibrations along the length of the molecule-kinetic energy and potential energy. Thus, it contributes two degrees of freedom. Thus vibrational energy $=2 \times \frac{1}{2} k_B T=k_B T$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The specific heat of helium at constant volume is 12.6 $\mathrm{Jmol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$. The specific heat of helium at constant pressure in $\mathrm{Jmol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$ is about (Assume the temperature of the gas is moderate, universal gas constant, $R=8.314 \mathrm{Jmol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$ )
questions.solution:
As $C_V=12.6 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$ is given in SI unit
So, $R=8314 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$
Then, $C_p=C_V+R=12.6+8.314=21$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
A thermally insulated vessel with nitrogen gas at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is moving with a velocity of $100 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. If the vessel is stopped suddenly, then the percentage change in the pressure of the gas is nearly
(assume entire loss in $\mathrm{KE}$ of the gas is given as heat to gas and $R=8.3 \mathrm{Jmol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$ )
questions.solution:
Let there are $n$ moles of $N_2$ gas in the cylinder.
Assuming all of $K, E$ appears in form of heat, $n\left(\frac{1}{2} M v^2\right)=\frac{f}{2} n R \Delta T$
Here, $M=28 \mathrm{~g}=28 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~kg}, f=5$
Increment in pressure due to change of temperature is
Also, $\Delta p=\frac{n R \Delta T}{V}$
So, $\frac{\Delta p}{p}=\frac{\left(\frac{n R \Delta T}{V}\right)}{\left(\frac{n R T}{V}\right)}=\frac{n R \Delta T}{n R T} \Rightarrow \frac{\Delta p}{p}=\frac{n M v^2}{f n R T}=\frac{M v^2}{f R T}$
So, percentage change in pressure is,
$$
\therefore \frac{\Delta p}{p} \times 100=\frac{28 \times 10^{-3} \times 100 \times 100}{5 \times 8.3 \times 300} \times 100=2.25 \%
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: A flask contains hydrogen and oxygen in the ratio of $2: 1$ by mass at temperature $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The ratio of average kinetic energy per molecule of hydrogen and oxygen respectively is :
questions.solution:
Average kinetic energy for diatomic gases
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{av}}=\frac{5}{2} \mathrm{kT} \\
& \therefore \frac{\left(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{av}}\right)_{\mathrm{H}}}{\left(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{av}}\right)_{\mathrm{O}}}=\frac{(27+273)}{(27+273)}=1
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: One mole of a monoatomic ideal gas undergoes a quasistatic process, which is depicted by a straight line joining points $\left(V_{0}, T_{0}\right)$ and $\left(2 V_{0}, 3 T_{0}\right)$ in a ${V} \cdot T$ diagram. What is the value of the heat capacity of the gas at the point $\left(V_{0}, T_{0}\right) ?$
questions.solution:
Heat capacity of an ideal gas in a thermodynamic process,
$\because$ Ideal gas is monoatomic,
$$
\begin{aligned}
C_{V} &=\frac{f R}{2}=\frac{3 R}{2} \\
C_{\text {process }} &=C_{V}+\frac{p}{n} \cdot \frac{d V}{d T} \\
&=\frac{3}{2} R+\frac{p}{n} \cdot \frac{V_{0}}{2 T} \\
\therefore C_{\text {process }}=\frac{3}{2} R+\frac{n R T}{n 2 T} &[\because p V=n R T] \\
=& \frac{3}{2} R+\frac{R}{2}=2 R
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Graph of specific heat at constant volume for a monoatomic gas is
questions.solution:
According to first law of thermodynamics
$$
\Delta Q=\Delta U+P \Delta V
$$
If $\Delta Q$ is absorbed at constant volume, $\Delta V=0$
$$
C_V=\left(\frac{\Delta Q}{\Delta T}\right)_V=\left(\frac{\Delta U}{\Delta T}\right)_V=\frac{\Delta U}{\Delta T}
$$
for an ideal monoatomic gas
$$
\frac{\Delta U}{\Delta T}=\frac{3}{2} R ; C_V=\frac{3}{2} R
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A diatomic gas consisting of rigid molecules is at a temperature of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>87</mn><mo> </mo><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. If the moment of inertia of the rotating diatomic rigid molecule is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>76</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>39</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mi>gcm</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> then the rms angular speed of the molecule is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo></math>Boltzmann constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>38</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>23</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mi>JK</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
Recall the formula of the kinetic energy of one molecule in terms of Boltzmann constant and absolute temperature,

K.E.=12KT, for two rigid molecules, it will be,

K.E.=2×12KT=KT, if I is a moment of inertia of body and ω is the angular velocity then,

KT=12Iω2⇒ω=2KTI=2×1.38×10-2387+2732.76×10-39×10-7=6×1012 rad s-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: 3 moles of an ideal gas at a temperature of $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ are mixed with 2 moles of an ideal gas at a temperature $227^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, determine the equilibrium temperature of the mixture, assuming no loss of energy.
questions.solution:
Energy possessed by the ideal gas at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is
$$
E_{1}=3\left(\frac{3}{2} R \times 300\right)=\frac{2700 R}{2}
$$
Energy possessed by the ideal gas at $227^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is
$$
\mathrm{E}_{2}=2\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{R}}{2} \times 500\right)=1500 \mathrm{R}
$$
If $\mathrm{T}$ be the equilibrium temperature, of the mixture, then its energy will be
$$
\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{m}}=5\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{R} \mathrm{T}}{2}\right)
$$
Since, energy remains conserved,
$$
\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{m}}=\mathrm{E}_{1}+\mathrm{E}_{2}
$$
or $\quad 5\left(\frac{3 R T}{2}\right)=\frac{2700 R}{2}+1500 R$
or $\quad \mathrm{T}=380 \mathrm{~K}$ or $107^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A gaseous mixture consists of $16 \mathrm{~g}$ of helium and $16 \mathrm{~g}$ of oxygen. The ratio $C_{p} / C_{V}$ of the mixture is
questions.solution:
Mass of helium, $m_{1}=16 \mathrm{~g}$
Mass of oxygen, $m_{2}=16 \mathrm{~g}$
$\therefore$ Number of moles of He gas,
$$
n_{1}=\frac{m_{1}}{4}=\frac{16}{4}=4
$$
Number of moles of $\mathrm{O}_{2} \mathrm{gas}$,
$$
n_{2}=\frac{m_{2}}{32}=\frac{16}{32}=\frac{1}{2}
$$
We know that for the mixture,
$\begin{aligned} C_{V} &=\frac{n_{1} C_{V}+n_{2} C_{V_{2}}}{n_{1}+n_{2}}, \text { where } C_{V}=\frac{f R}{2} \\ \text { and } C_{p} &=\frac{n_{1} C_{p_{1}}+n_{2} C_{p_{2}}}{n_{1}+n_{2}}, \text { where } C_{p}=\left(\frac{f}{2}+1\right) R \end{aligned}$
For He gas, $f=3, n_{1}=4$
For $\mathrm{O}_{2}$ gas, $f=5, n_{2}=\frac{1}{2}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad \frac{C_{p}}{C_{V}} &=\frac{n_{1} C_{p_{1}}+n_{2} C_{p_{2}}}{n_{1} C_{V_{1}}+n_{2} C_{V_{2}}} \\
&=\frac{4 \times\left(\frac{3}{2}+1\right) R+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{5}{2}+1\right) R}{4 \times \frac{3}{2} R+\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{5}{2} R} \\
&=\frac{\left(4 \times \frac{5}{2} R\right)+\left(\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{7}{2} R\right)}{\left(4 \times \frac{3}{2} R\right)+\left(\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{5}{2} R\right)}=\frac{47}{29} \\
&=1.62
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A gas has molar heat capacity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>37</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>55</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>mol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>, in this process <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mi>T</mi><mo>=</mo></math> constant. The number of degree of freedom of the molecule of gas is
questions.solution:
Given, C=37.55 J mol-1 K-1

Also, PT= constant K

According to ideal gas equation, PV=RT

⇒   p=RTV      .....i

Putting the value of p in Eq. (i), we get

RTV×T=K   ⇒   V=RT2K     .....ii

On differentiating above equation both sides, we get

dVdT=2RTK....iii
But TK=1p     fromEq.  i
Hence, Eq. iiibecomes

dVdT=2 Rp
So, C=CV+pdVdT
or C=CV+p×2 Rp=CV+2 R
or CV=C-2 R      ...iv

As CV=nR2, where n= number of degrees of freedom.
Putting the value of CV in Eq. (iv), we get

nR2=C-2 R

n=2 C-2 RR=2 37.55-2×8.38.3=5.048≈5
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
